# Project Progress

## Current Stage

prover

## Stages
- [x] init
- [x] autoformalize
- [ ] prover
- [ ] polish

## End-state overview

**Zero inline `sorry` in the dependency cone of the protected declaration
`AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage`, kernel-only axioms, 0 project
axioms.** Sole goal: close the Čech computation of higher direct images
(`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`) and its cone. Full framing in STRATEGY.md.

## Iter-025 context — both iter-024 bottlenecks closed; dispatch the now-unblocked `injective_cech_acyclic` frontier; absolute-Hⁿ realization decided (Ext)

- **iter-024 ran both lanes (+21 axiom-clean decls, 0 new sorries, build GREEN) — BOTH named targets landed:**
  - **`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`** (FreePresheafComplex) — the free Čech complex augmentation is a
    quasi-iso / free resolution of `O_𝒰`. The project's longest churn (target absent iters 020–023);
    landed via Route B (arrow-iso transfer along `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`, NOT Homotopy repackaging).
  - **`ses_cech_h1`** (CechBridge) — Čech-H¹ sheaf-gluing surjectivity (full Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).
- **This iter (plan phase):**
  - **Coverage debt cleared 32→0** (blueprint-writer `coverage-025`): all prover-created helpers bundled
    into `\lean{}` lists of `lem:ses_cech_h1` / `lem:cech_engine_complex` / `lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso`
    / `lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty`. `archon dag-query unmatched` = 0. `\restriction` macro fixed.
  - **Absolute `Hⁿ(U,F)` realization DECIDED** (mathlib-analogist `abscohom-025`): `Hᵖ(U,F) :=
    Ext^p(𝒪_U, F|_U)` via Mathlib `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext`. The suspected `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H`
    route was **REFUTED** (absent from Mathlib); `Functor.rightDerived` has injective vanishing but **no
    LES**. Ext is the ONLY route with the 01EO long-exact-sequence off-the-shelf. Blueprint
    `def:absolute_cohomology` + 6 `\mathlibok` anchors added (writer `extcohom-025`); 01EO/02KG proofs
    rewired to cite the Ext LES + `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective`. `analogies/absolute-cohomology.md`.
  - **blueprint-reviewer `iter025` (HARD GATE): CLEARS `lem:injective_cech_acyclic`** (statement faithful
    to Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`, proof formalize-ready, both gate deps `\leanok`); all 6 Ext
    anchors verified sound. One must-fix (undefined `\restriction` macro) — FIXED by planner.

## Current Objectives

ONE frontier prover lane this iter (the genuine ready frontier — both its gates landed iter-024). HARD-GATE
cleared (chapter `chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`).

1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechBridge.lean`** — scaffold + build the `injective_cech_acyclic` declaration that **does not yet exist**, plus fix the two stale module docstrings. [prover-mode: mathlib-build]
   - **P3b bridge — THE frontier (both gates now in hand).** Build
     **`AlgebraicGeometry.injective_cech_acyclic`** (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks
     `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): for any open cover `𝒰` of `X` and an **injective** `O_X`-module `I`,
     the section Čech cohomology of `I` vanishes in positive degrees (`Ȟᵖ(𝒰,I)=0` for `p>0`), with `Ȟ⁰ = I(U)`.
     The Lean target is the positive-degree `IsZero` homology (or `QuasiIso`/exactness) of the section Čech
     complex of `I`, matching the blueprint statement + the `injective_toPresheafOfModules` half already in
     `PresheafCech.lean` (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.injective_cech_acyclic, AlgebraicGeometry.injective_toPresheafOfModules}`).
   - **Proof — ONE-STEP op-transport assembly (all inputs in-file/imported, all `\leanok`):**
     (1) **Part 1 (already available):** an injective `O_X`-module is injective as a presheaf of modules —
        `injective_toPresheafOfModules` (PresheafCech) via `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` applied to the
        `Mod ↪ PMod` right adjoint `sheafificationAdjunction`. Hence `Hom_{O_X}(-, I)` is exact on `PMod`.
     (2) **Part 2 (the assembly):** `cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` is a quasi-iso (**`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`**,
        iter-024, `lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`). Take its opposite via `HomologicalComplex.op`; apply
        `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` — it preserves the quasi-iso because `I` is injective-as-presheaf, via
        **`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`** (`lem:hom_into_injective_exact`, already in CechBridge).
        Transport source & target of the mapped-opposite quasi-iso across **`homCechComplexMapOpIso`**
        (`lem:cech_complex_op_identification`) and **`sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`**
        (`lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso`) ⇒ the section Čech complex augmentation of `I` is a quasi-iso
        onto the degree-0-concentrated `O_𝒰[0]` image ⇒ positive-degree homology vanishes, `Ȟ⁰ = I(U)`.
     Every named ingredient is built and `\leanok`; this is the "one-step op-transport" both iter-024 task
     results flagged as immediate. If a transport step snags on a defeq-carrier / functor-coercion `rw`
     mismatch (the recurring project obstacle), use `erw` + `refine (term).trans ?_` (close up to defeq) per
     the KB; land what you can and hand off a precise decomposition — do NOT pin a sorry (mathlib-build:
     all-or-nothing, no sorry in output).
   - **Also fix the 2 stale CechBridge module docstrings** (lean-auditor `iter024` major): the header still
     calls `ses_cech_h1` "(planned)" and `injective_cech_acyclic` "gated on Lane-1" — both now FALSE. Update
     to reflect both as built.
   - Blueprint: `chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`,
     `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`, `lem:cech_complex_op_identification`,
     `lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso`, `lem:hom_into_injective_exact`, `lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`,
     `lem:injective_of_adjoint`, `lem:mod_pmod_adjunction`).

## Off-limits / not this iter
- `FreePresheafComplex.lean` — **DONE** (`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` landed iter-024; 0 sorries). Imported
  read-only by Lane 1. No prover.
- `CechAcyclic.lean` — **P3 section form DONE iter-022** (`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`). The old line-110
  `CechAcyclic.affine` sorry is superseded/authorized. No prover.
- `PresheafCech.lean` — section side DONE (incl. `injective_toPresheafOfModules`). No prover (imported by Lane 1).
- `HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean` — P5a 01XJ LEAF DONE (resolution form). No prover.
- `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` — P5b assembly BLOCKED (needs P3+P3b+P5a). `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`
  signature + path FROZEN (protected). No prover.
- `AcyclicResolution.lean`, `HigherDirectImage.lean` — DONE (0 sorries). No prover.

## Next iter plan — ordered
1. Process the `injective_cech_acyclic` output (lvb on CechBridge; lean-auditor; progress-critic). Bundle any
   new helpers into `\lean{}` lists (keep `unmatched` at 0).
2. **If `injective_cech_acyclic` lands**: the whole 01EO→02KG endgame opens. Dispatch the **Ext-based
   absolute-cohomology scaffold** — build `def:absolute_cohomology` infra (`Hᵖ(U,F):=Ext^p(𝒪_U,F|_U)` via
   `restrictFunctor` + `Abelian.Ext`, `H⁰=Γ` plumbing) as a `mathlib-build` lane (its own file, e.g.
   `AbsoluteCohomology.lean`), THEN `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO, dimension shift via the Ext LES) →
   `affine_serre_vanishing` (02KG). 01EO is large (effort ~3.5k) — consider effort-breaking it first.
3. **If `injective_cech_acyclic` partials**: read its handoff; the assembly is mechanical op-transport, so a
   partial likely means a defeq-carrier snag — effort-break the snagging transport step and re-dispatch.
4. P5b Route-A inputs (`cechAugmented_exact`, `cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic`, `open_immersion_pushforward_comp`):
   gated on `affine_serre_vanishing` + the relative-nerve↔section bridge (general qcoh `F≅~ΓF`, Stacks 01I8).

## Deferred / standing notes
- **Absolute `Hⁿ(U,F)` = `Ext^p(𝒪_U,F|_U)` (DECIDED iter-025)** — Mathlib anchors: `Abelian.Ext`,
  `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` (LES), `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (keep injective `I` as the 2nd Ext
  arg ⇒ no "restriction-preserves-injectives" needed), `Ext.homEquiv₀`/`addEquiv₀` (`H⁰=Hom`),
  `HasExt.standard` (unconditional), `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor` (sections over `U`). Reversal: Ext
  universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), NEVER Route β.
- **General-qcoh `F≅~(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8)** — the residual reduction of arbitrary qcoh `F` on an affine to
  the tilde case; consumed by the 02KG/02KE general-F assembly + `cechAugmented_exact`. Build when reached.
- **FreeCechEngine/CombinatorialCech duplication** (lean-auditor MAJOR, carried): kept; honestly
  blueprinted (`lem:free_cech_engine`). Revisit as a polish-phase refactor once P3b closes.
- Verified-present Mathlib (carried, re-verify before use — Mathlib bumps rename): the op-transport bridge
  ingredients `HomologicalComplex.op`, `preadditiveYoneda`, `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`,
  `homCechComplexMapOpIso`, `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` — ALL already built in-project (CechBridge) +

`CechHigherDirectImage.lean:679`). `injective_cech_acyclic`'s two gates are now both in hand → it is the
genuine ready frontier. Coverage debt had grown to 32 unmatched `lean_aux` helpers (both iter-024 lvb
reports flagged this).

## What I did this iter (plan phase)
1. Processed iter-024 results (task_done += the two landings; task_pending rewritten: free side DONE,
   `ses_cech_h1` DONE, `injective_cech_acyclic` promoted to frontier).

   `lem:ses_cech_h1` / `lem:cech_engine_complex` / `lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso` /
   `lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty`. `archon dag-query unmatched` 32→0, no broken `\uses`.

   next obstacle gating 01EO + the P5a consumers. **Headline: `Hᵖ(U,F):=Ext^p(𝒪_U,F|_U)` via Mathlib
   `Abelian.Ext`.** The strategy's suspected `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H`+forget route was **REFUTED** (no
   AddCommGrp-valued sheaf cohomology exists in Mathlib); `Functor.rightDerived` has injective vanishing
   but **no LES**. Ext is the only route shipping the 01EO LES off-the-shelf
   (`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact`, `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective`, `Ext.homEquiv₀`, `HasExt.standard`).
   → `analogies/absolute-cohomology.md`.

   restriction anchors; rewired `lem:affine_serre_vanishing` + `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` to `\uses`

## Decision made

### D1 — Adopt `Ext` as the absolute-cohomology realization (the analogist's ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB verdict).
**Chosen:** `Hᵖ(U,F):=Ext^p(𝒪_U,F|_U)` via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext`. **Why:** it is the *only* route
where Mathlib ships the load-bearing 01EO long exact sequence off the shelf — the dimension-shift proof of
01EO is unbuildable without a sheaf-cohomology LES, and both other routes force a multi-hundred-LOC
from-scratch build (β = the whole abelian-sheaf cohomology theory, which is *absent*; α = re-deriving the

2896:  \mathlibok

2912:  \mathlibok
2928:  \label{lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib}
2930:  \mathlibok
=== def:absolute_cohomology block ===
  \label{def:absolute_cohomology}
  \uses{lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib, lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib,
    lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib, lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib,
    lem:hasext_standard_mathlib, lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib}
  \textit{Realization of absolute sheaf cohomology in the module-sheaf category.}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(U \subseteq
  X\) an open subscheme with open immersion \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\). Write
  \(\mathcal{F}|_U\) for the restriction of \(\mathcal{F}\) along \(j\) — the image of
  \(\mathcal{F}\) under the Mathlib restriction functor \(j^\ast :
  X.\mathrm{Modules} \to U.\mathrm{Modules}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib} — and \(\mathcal{O}_U\) for the
  structure sheaf of \(U\) regarded as a module over itself. The \emph{absolute sheaf
  cohomology of \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(U\)} is defined to be the Ext of the structure
  sheaf in the abelian category \(U.\mathrm{Modules} = \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_U)\) of
  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules:
  \[
    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\; \operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_U)}
      \bigl(\mathcal{O}_U,\; \mathcal{F}|_U\bigr),
    \qquad p \geq 0,
  \]
  equivalently \(\operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U,
  \mathcal{F})\). Here \(\operatorname{Ext}\) is Mathlib's Ext bifunctor
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib}) on the abelian category
  \(U.\mathrm{Modules}\); the \(\mathrm{HasExt}\) structure it requires is available
  unconditionally by Lemma~\ref{lem:hasext_standard_mathlib}. The three structural facts
  the downstream dimension-shift arguments consume are then exactly the corresponding
  Mathlib results:
  \begin{enumerate}
  \item \textbf{Degree zero is global sections.} In degree \(0\), Ext is the Hom-group,
    \(\operatorname{Ext}^0(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}|_U) \cong
    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}|_U) = \Gamma(U,
    \mathcal{F})\), by the degree-zero Ext comparison
    (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}); so \(H^0(U, \mathcal{F}) \cong
    \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\).
  \item \textbf{Injective vanishing.} When the \emph{second} Ext argument is an
    injective module \(\mathcal{J}\), one has \(\operatorname{Ext}^{n+1}(-,
    \mathcal{J}) = 0\) by Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}. Placing an
    injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) in that second-argument role
    (via the \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) form over \(\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)) yields
    \(H^{n+1}(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) directly, with no appeal to restriction preserving
    injectives.
  \item \textbf{Long exact sequence.} For a short exact sequence \(0 \to \mathcal{F}
    \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) of modules and the \emph{fixed} first
    argument \(\mathcal{O}_U\), the covariant Ext long exact sequence
    (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) is the long exact sequence of the
    groups \(H^p(U, -)\), with connecting maps given by composition with the Ext class
    of the extension.
  \end{enumerate}
\end{definition}

The `def:absolute_cohomology` block is detailed, but it leaves a real design fork unresolved (the `j_!` extension-by-zero form vs the `restrictFunctor` form — which determines whether injective-vanishing is free or needs "restriction preserves injectives"). The analogist flagged `j_!` availability as unverified. That makes a cold Ext-scaffold lane risky **this** iter — it confirms D2 (one frontier lane).
